description: Map Editor & Polish — Developer-facing map editor, 2D map overlay, balance tuning passes, edge case fixes, visual polish, and final quality gate.

userStories.title: Map editor mode — toggle, camera, and core module
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to enter a map editor mode so I can design levels visually.
acceptanceCriteria: Ctrl+E toggles map editor mode (only in import.meta.env.DEV builds)
Entering editor: game pauses (Game.pause()), camera switches to free mode via CameraController.setMode('free')
Exiting editor: game resumes (Game.resume()), camera returns to follow mode
Editor does NOT destroy existing game entities — they persist while editing
src/maps/MapEditor.ts exposes: enable(), disable(), isActive(): boolean, getMapData(): MapData, loadMapData(data: MapData)
getMapData() serializes current map state into a valid MapData object
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Map editor — terrain placement tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to place and modify terrain blocks so I can shape the map geometry.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ui/MapEditorUI.ts renders a side panel with tool palette when editor is active
Tool palette contains: Grass block, Dirt block, Stone block, Water zone
Select tool then click on map: terrain block entity created at grid-snapped position (1u grid, Math.round())
Terrain blocks have adjustable height via side panel input (0.5u to 5u in 0.5u steps)
Click existing terrain block: selects it, side panel shows editable properties (type dropdown, height input, position display)
Delete key removes selected terrain block entity
Water zone tool: click-drag to define rectangular water area, depth input in side panel
Ghost preview of block shown at mouse position before placement
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Map editor — entity placement tools
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to place spawn points, resource nodes, and special zones on the map.
acceptanceCriteria: Entity tools added to tool palette: Player Spawn, Cat Spawn, Resource Node (Grass/Sticks/Water dropdown), Hidden Terrain Zone, Yarn Pickup
Player Spawn: only one allowed on map — placing a new one moves the existing one
Cat Spawn: place multiple markers, each shows as a distinct colored icon
Resource Node: select resource type from dropdown, place on map, respawn time editable in side panel
Hidden Terrain Zone: click-drag to define rectangular area, height editable in side panel
Yarn Pickup: place collectible marker, yarn amount editable in side panel (default 3)
All placed entities show as distinct colored markers/shapes distinguishable from terrain
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Map editor — move, delete, and keyboard shortcuts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to move and delete placed objects so I can iterate on map layouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Move tool: click entity to select, drag to new position, release to place (grid-snapped to 1u)
Delete tool: click entity to remove it from the map
Keyboard shortcuts when editor active: V for move tool, X for delete tool, 1-9 for tool palette selection
Selected entity has highlighted border or outline for visual feedback
Undo is explicitly NOT implemented (out of scope for MVP)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Map editor — save and load JSON files
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to save map layouts as JSON files and load them so that maps are portable.
acceptanceCriteria: Save button in editor UI: calls MapEditor.getMapData(), JSON.stringify() result, triggers browser download dialog
Filename auto-generated: map-[MapData.name]-[ISO timestamp].json
Load button: opens file picker, reads selected JSON file, validates it has required MapData fields, calls MapEditor.loadMapData(parsed)
Loaded map replaces current map entirely (all terrain/entity entities destroyed and recreated from loaded data)
Play button: calls MapManager.loadMap(mapEditor.getMapData()), then MapEditor.disable() — exits editor and plays on edited map
Invalid JSON file shows error message in editor UI (does not crash or corrupt state)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: 2D map overlay — overhead navigation view
userStories.description: As a player, I want to see an overhead 2D map so I can orient myself in the world.
acceptanceCriteria: src/game/NavigationOverlay.ts renders when M key pressed (InputManager GameAction.ToggleMap)
Uses a separate HTML5 canvas element overlaid on game (not Three.js), centered on screen, ~400x400px
Draws terrain cells as colored rectangles: Grass=green, Dirt=brown, Stone=gray, Water=blue
Hidden terrain NOT shown unless currently revealed by Curiosity Cat
Player icon: colored circle matching character color with arrow showing facing direction
Active cat positions: small colored squares at their world positions
Resource nodes: small dots colored by type (green=Grass, brown=Sticks, blue=Water)
Map scales terrain grid to fit the overlay canvas size
Pressing M again or Escape closes the overlay
Game continues running behind overlay (NOT paused) but movement input consumed while overlay open
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Edge case fixes — stuck states and death respawn
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all known edge cases handled gracefully so the game never enters an unrecoverable state.
acceptanceCriteria: Player cannot get stuck in terrain — PhysicsEngine push-out resolution if clipping detected (raycast check + position correction)
Player cannot walk out of map bounds — collision walls at all 4 map edges (created by MapManager.loadMap())
Cat summon inside collision geometry: auto-raise attempt (move up 0.5u, retry up to 3 times) — if all fail, summon returns null with console warning
Cat dismissed while player standing on it: player falls normally via gravity (no void or clip)
Health reaches 0 (from oxygen drain): player respawns at map spawn point with 3 hearts and full oxygen (no death screen — Zen Engagement pillar)
Inventory full + gathering attempt: clear 'Inventory Full' text feedback near player, gathering does not start
Save while swimming: on load, if saved position is inside water zone, SwimmingState component added correctly
Rapid input (hold multiple keys, mash jump, spam cat summon): no crashes, no inconsistent state — input debouncing prevents duplicate actions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Visual polish — shadows, animations, and effects
userStories.description: As a developer, I want visual refinements that make the game feel more alive without adding art assets.
acceptanceCriteria: Ground plane shadows: player and cat entities cast elliptical shadows onto ground plane (add shadow plane mesh below each dynamic entity via SceneManager)
Terrain color variation: slight random tint variation per terrain cell (e.g., grass cells vary +/-10% in hue/brightness)
Water zone: semi-transparent blue (opacity 0.4) with subtle opacity pulse animation (0.35-0.45 over 2s cycle)
Cat summon effect: entity scales from 0 to 1 over 0.2 seconds when created (Transform.scale interpolation in RenderSystem)
Cat dismiss effect: entity scales from 1 to 0 over 0.2 seconds before entity destruction
Gather progress bar: smooth CSS width transition (not jerky step increments)
Hidden terrain reveal: opacity fade from 0 to 1 over 0.5 seconds (not instant snap)
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Movement balance pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to verify all movement values feel correct and adjust if needed.
acceptanceCriteria: Walk speed (4.5 u/s) verified: map traversal feels purposeful, not sluggish or too fast
Acceleration (0.3s) verified: movement start feels responsive but weighted
Deceleration (0.2s) verified: stopping feels controlled, not slippery
Jump impulse (3.5u) verified: reaches intended 1.2u apex and intended platforms
Coyote time (5 frames) verified: jumping after walking off edges works reliably
Jump buffer (5 frames) verified: rapid repeated jumps feel responsive
Air control (70%) verified: mid-air steering works without feeling like ice physics
Swimming speeds verified: surface 3.2, dive 2.0, ascend 2.5 u/s feel appropriate
Any values adjusted documented with comment in config.ts explaining rationale
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cat and resource balance pass
userStories.description: As a developer, I want cat costs/effects and resource gathering tuned to GDD feel targets.
acceptanceCriteria: Loaf (1 yarn) height verified: stacking 2-3 reaches elevated platforms correctly
Zoomies (2 yarn) verified: 2x speed and 8s duration feels impactful but balanced
Curiosity Cat (2 yarn) verified: 5u radius reveals enough, 20s is sufficient to explore
Pounce (3 yarn) verified: 3.5u launch reliably reaches elevated platforms
Starting yarn (10) verified: enough to experiment with all cat types in one session
Cat limit (3) verified: creates meaningful placement decisions
Grass gather time (1.5s) verified: feels snappy per GDD 'instant gratification' target
Resource respawn timers verified: Grass 30s, Sticks 45s, Water 60s feel balanced
Inventory cap (10) verified: creates tension without frustration
Any adjusted values documented with comments in config.ts or cat definition files
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Final quality gate — lint, typecheck, build, integration verification
userStories.description: As a developer, I need all quality checks passing so the MVP is shippable.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm lint passes with zero warnings
pnpm typecheck passes with zero errors
pnpm build succeeds and produces optimized bundle
No console errors on clean startup in browser
Game maintains 60 FPS with test map fully populated (all resource nodes, 3 active cats, swimming active)
Auth flow works end-to-end: sign in -> game, sign out -> title screen, refresh -> session persists
Save/load round trip verified: play 1 minute, close tab, reopen, state matches (position, yarn, inventory, cats)
All 4 cat types functional and produce intended effects
Swimming + oxygen system functional (enter water, dive, oxygen drains, surface, exit)
Debug menu opens with Ctrl+D, all tabs functional, changes apply immediately
Map editor: Ctrl+E opens editor, place terrain, save JSON, load JSON, play on edited map
Typecheck passes
